title: Humea corymbulosa
taxonomy: Humea corymbulosa is classified in the family Sapindaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the soapberry family. This family includes around 140 species of trees and shrubs. Humea corymbulosa is native to tropical Asia, specifically in Sri Lanka. The classification of Humea corymbulosa is further detailed within the genus Humea, which is a group of flowering plants. The exact classification of Humea corymbulosa is further confirmed as genus: Humea, family: Sapindaceae.
uses: Humea corymbulosa has been reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, wounds, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of its medicinal uses. However, its potential as a natural remedy for certain health conditions is being explored by scientists.
